name: Skagen
notes: Solid-state AESA; NATO primary air-defence sensor covering northern Denmark. Acquired 1995; being replaced by Lockheed Martin TPY-4.

name: Bornholm
notes: Martello stacked-beam 3D radar, tower-mounted, NATO-funded. Guards the Baltic approach from Bornholm. Slated for replacement 2025-2029.

name: Skrydstrup Air Base
notes: Transportable AESA derivative of the FPS-117 family; frequency-agile (100 channels). Detects fighters, high-altitude ballistic trajectories and low-flying cruise missiles.

name: Aalborg Air Base
notes: Airfield/terminal primary surveillance radar (2016 contract). Range/azimuth only - altitude comes from the co-located Mode-S secondary radar, not this sensor.

name: Karup Air Base
notes: Airfield/terminal primary surveillance radar (2016 contract). Range/azimuth only - altitude comes from the co-located Mode-S secondary radar, not this sensor.
